tasks.name: Git Backup
tasks.system_prompt: You are an automation agent responsible for performing git backups and notifying the user of results. Execute tasks reliably and report outcomes clearly.
tasks.prompt:
Execute the git-backup skill by running the backup script at /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py, then use the notify-user skill to send a notification with the backup result.

Steps:
1. Clean up all of the task history if the last run is successful. This is critical to keep our context minimal without losing important details!
2. Run: python /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py
3. Capture the output/result
4. Send notification using: python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "<backup result message>" --title "Git Backup Result" --priority 3
Always report whether the backup succeeded or failed in the notification.
tasks.last_result:

tasks.name: Blog Ideas
tasks.system_prompt: You are a blog topic researcher for Able.SG, a tech leadership blog by Michael Bui - a Singapore-based engineering leader who runs an extensive homelab, builds multi-agent AI systems, and leads AI transformation at a large retail organization.
tasks.prompt:
This task has 4 steps. Follow them in exact order.

== STEP 1: Extract Published Blog Titles and Date Range ==

Run this shell command to get published (non-draft) blog post titles AND the search date range:

bash -c '
REPO_DIR="/sources/github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG"
if [ -d "$REPO_DIR/.git" ]; then
  git -C "$REPO_DIR" pull --quiet 2>/dev/null
else
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$REPO_DIR")"
  git clone https://github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG.git "$REPO_DIR" --depth 1 --quiet 2>/dev/null
fi
echo "=== PUBLISHED POSTS ==="
count=0
for f in "$REPO_DIR"/content/english/posts/*.md; do
  draft=$(grep -m1 "^draft:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | tr -d " " | cut -d: -f2)
  [ "$draft" = "true" ] && continue
  title=$(grep -m1 "^title:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | sed "s/^title: *//;s/\"//g")
  if [ -n "$title" ]; then
    echo "- $title"
    count=$((count + 1))
  fi
done
if [ "$count" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "WARNING: No blog titles found. Check if /sources mount is available."
fi
echo ""
echo "=== SEARCH DATE RANGE ==="
THIS_MONTH="$(date +"%B %Y")"
PREV_MONTH="$(date -d "last month" +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null || date -v-1m +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null)"
echo "Search for topics trending in: $PREV_MONTH and $THIS_MONTH"
echo "⚠️ CRITICAL: All search queries must end with \" since $PREV_MONTH\" keywords"
'

Save the output. The PUBLISHED_POSTS section lists posts to avoid duplicating. The SEARCH DATE RANGE gives you two months to include in your search queries.

== STEP 2: Research Topics (run at least 7 web searches) ==

You must perform AT LEAST 7 separate web searches using search_engine before proposing topics. Design your own search queries, but follow these rules:

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Minimum 7 searches total, each with a DIFFERENT query
- At least 2 searches for Leadership topics (people management, engineering culture, AI adoption, trust, growth)
- At least 3 searches for Technology topics (agentic AI, local LLMs, homelab, MCP, clean architecture, DORA)
- At least 2 searches for Life topics (productivity, work-life balance, continuous learning, personal growth)
- Each search must approach the pillar from a DIFFERENT angle (e.g. for Technology: one about trends, one about community discussions, one about practical implementations)
- Include the month names and year from the SEARCH DATE RANGE (Step 1 output) in your queries to limit results to the last 2 months
- If a search returns interesting leads, do follow-up searches to go deeper

Execute all searches one by one, then analyze the results against Michael's expertise below.

MICHAEL'S UNIQUE EXPERTISE (real hands-on experience):
- Multi-agent orchestration (built 2 production systems: enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report)
- Agent Zero framework (runs 11 specialist agents, built custom theme plugin, skills for Gmail/Jira/GChat via CDP browser)
- AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server (local LLM inference with ROCm/llama.cpp)
- Retail media networks / ad-tech (scaled from $1M to $10M+ GMV at NTUC FairPrice)
- DORA metrics, SLO management, engineering culture (Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98%)
- AI adoption at organizational scale (drove 28% to 100% department-wide, 2/3 building agentic AI)
- Clean architecture in Go microservices (SOLID, 12-factor app, SonarCloud quality gates)
- Terraform IaC for homelab (Proxmox VMs, Docker containers, NFS)
- RAG evaluation (ColPali multi-vector vs sparse vector, error rates, normalization)
- Cost optimization ($100K+ annual cloud savings: Datadog $70K + GCP + SOLR legacy removal)
- Team leadership (up to 14 cross-functional members, mentored 5 engineers to team lead)
- Homelab migration (Kubernetes + ArgoCD + Cloudflare to Docker + Terraform + Traefik, 30% power reduction)
- Prompt engineering and context engineering for production agentic systems
- Vendor evaluation (CitrusAd, Mirakl, MoEngage, Algolia, theTradeDesk, OSMOS, Segment)

TOPIC SELECTION RULES:
1. Do NOT suggest topics overlapping with PUBLISHED_POSTS from Step 1
2. Each topic MUST connect to at least one item from Michael's expertise above
3. Prioritize topics where personal experience gives an angle generic AI content cannot replicate
4. No purely theoretical topics - must have a hands-on component
5. Target audience: mid-to-senior engineers, tech leads, engineering managers in APAC/Singapore
6. Each topic should be writable as 800-1200 words

After all searches are complete, decide on 5-7 topics. For each, pick a TITLE and a SCORE (1-10, composite of search demand + uniqueness + freshness). Keep these in mind for Step 3.

== STEP 3: Save Topics to File, Then Notify ==

⚠️ CRITICAL: This step has two parts, you MUST execute BOTH before moving to Step 4.

PART A - SAVE TO FILE:
Create the output directory and save all topics with full details to a markdown file. Run:

mkdir -p "workdir/Blog Ideas"

Then write a file at workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_YYYY-MM-DD.md (replace YYYY-MM-DD with today's date). The file must follow this structure:

# Blog Topic Ideas - YYYY-MM-DD

## 1. [TITLE]
- **Pillar:** [Leadership / Technology / Life]
- **Score:** [X/10]
- **Angle:** [3-5 sentences on what makes Michael's take unique]
- **Trending Signal:** [detailed findings from web search with links to actual sources]
- **Related Expertise:** [which items from Michael's expertise list this connects to]

## 2. [TITLE]
(same structure for each topic)

PART B - SEND NOTIFICATION:
After saving the file, send a push notification with titles and scores. Execute:

python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "Blog Topics: (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | ... Check Agent Zero chat for details." --title "Blog Topic Ideas" --priority 3

Replace each SCORE and TITLE with actual values from your topics. Include ALL topics separated by " | ".

Execute both Part A and Part B now before proceeding to Step 4.

== STEP 4: Write Detailed Output ==

Now write the full details for each topic in this format:

[NUMBER]. TITLE: [proposed blog title]
ANGLE: [3-5 sentences - what personal experience or data makes Michael's take unique]
TRENDING SIGNAL: [what you found in web search - specific subreddit, HN thread, trend, or event, be detailed and verbose with links to actual references]
SCORE: [1-10]
tasks.last_result:
**Blog Topic Recommendations** 📝

---

## 1. The Hidden Cost of Cloud LLM Inference: Why a $3K Strix Halo Server Beats $50K Annual API Bills
- **Pillar:** Technology
- **Score:** 9/10
- **Angle:** Michael's hands-on experience with AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory server (ROCm/llama.cpp) allows him to provide real cost breakdowns, not theoretical comparisons. He can share actual electricity costs ($0.05/hour), token throughput metrics, and the hidden operational overhead of cloud APIs that generic analyses miss.
- **Trending Signal:** 
  - "Local LLMs vs Cloud APIs: 2026 Total Cost of Ownership Analysis" (sitepoint.com) - Direct TCO comparison showing self-hosted wins at scale
  - "AMD Strix Halo AI Workstations Bring LLMs to the Desktop" (insighttechdaily.com, Apr 2026) - Hardware trend accelerating in March-April 2026
  - Jimmy Claw's blog: "Why we are buying a Strix Halo — my path to self-hosted inference" showing marginal cost per token is electricity
  - Multiple search results show clear shift from cloud to self-hosted in Q1 2026
- **Related Expertise:** AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server, Cost optimization ($100K+ annual cloud savings), Homelab migration (30% power reduction)

---

## 2. Building Agentic AI Governance: Guardrails That Don't Stifle Innovation
- **Pillar:** Technology
- **Score:** 8/10
- **Angle:** Michael built two production multi-agent systems (enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report) and runs Agent Zero with 11 specialist agents. He can share practical guardrail patterns (allowlists, audit logging at protocol boundaries) from actual deployments, not just theoretical frameworks.
- **Trending Signal:**
  - Vellum AI's "Agentic Workflows in 2026: The ultimate guide" (Mar 2026) - Emerging design patterns and challenges
  - Neo4j's agentic architecture article (Mar 2026) - Emphasizes need for guardrails without stifling innovation
  - Reddit r/AI_Agents discussion on MCP (Mar 2026): "MCP in 2026 - it's complicated" highlighting brittleness in orchestration layers
  - Arxiv survey on agentic AI architectures emphasizing validation and consistency layers
- **Related Expertise:** Multi-agent orchestration (built 2 production systems), Agent Zero framework (runs 11 specialist agents), Prompt engineering and context engineering for production agentic systems

---

## 3. The AI Adoption Paradox: Why 70% of Engineers Adopt Tools But Only 28% Use Them Daily
- **Pillar:** Leadership
- **Score:** 9/10
- **Angle:** Michael drove department-wide AI adoption from 28% to 100% at a large retail org. He can share the specific change management strategies that converted skeptics, not just high-level frameworks. His hands-on experience with measuring weekly usage and improving workflows regularly is unique.
- **Trending Signal:**
  - Ideaware's "AI Adoption Risk | When Your Engineering Team Won't Adapt" (Mar 17, 2026) - Notes engineering teams stalling adoption
  - McKinsey's "State of AI trust in 2026: Shifting to the agentic era" (Mar 25, 2026) - Highlights consequences as autonomy increases
  - Exceeds.ai data: High-performing teams achieve 60-70% weekly adoption when measuring usage regularly
  - HBR-sponsored "Four Trends in AI Experimentation, Adoption, and Transformation" (Mar 2026) - Lagging employee adoption as key barrier
- **Related Expertise:** AI adoption at organizational scale (drove 28% to 100% department-wide), Team leadership (up to 14 cross-functional members)

---

## 4. Beyond DORA: The Real Metrics That Predict Engineering Resilience in 2026
- **Pillar:** Leadership
- **Score:** 8/10
- **Angle:** Michael maintains Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98% at scale. He can share how he translates DORA metrics into actionable engineering culture changes, not just dashboards. His experience with SLO management and correlating metrics to team well-being is unique.
- **Trending Signal:**
  - Programming-helper's "DORA Metrics 2026: How Elite Software Delivery Teams Measure Performance" (Mar 2026) - Platform engineering + AI-assisted development as leading edge
  - Devopsdigest article "The Top 10% of Engineering Teams Have Gone Beyond Dora Metrics" (Aug 2023, still referenced in 2026 discussions)
  - Exceeds.ai "DORA Metrics Engineering Effectiveness: AI Impact in 2026" - Five core metrics for measuring effectiveness
  - Lean.org's "Value-Stream Mapping Is Your Missing AI Superpower" (Feb 4, 2026) - Correlating metrics to organizational performance
- **Related Expertise:** DORA metrics, SLO management, engineering culture (Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98%)

---

## 5. The Engineer's New Career Path: From Code Writer to AI Orchestrator
- **Pillar:** Life
- **Score:** 7/10
- **Angle:** Michael mentored 5 engineers to team leads in the AI era. He can share concrete upskilling paths that worked, not generic advice. His experience with self-directed AI upskilling and measuring ROI on learning is unique.
- **Trending Signal:**
  - BCG's "AI Will Reshape More Jobs Than It Replaces" (2026) - Humans continue playing meaningful roles
  - WEF's "Software developers are the vanguard of how AI is redefining work" (Jan 2026) - Self-directed AI upskilling as key talent strategy
  - Reddit r/ArtificialIntelligence discussion (Mar 26, 2026): "The 'AI is replacing software engineers' narrative was a lie" with 2.3K votes
  - CNN article on AI impact (Apr 8, 2026) - Engineers scrambling to adjust but finding new roles emerging
- **Related Expertise:** Team leadership (mentored 5 engineers to team lead), Continuous learning mindset

---

## 6. Homelab Cost Optimization: How Docker + Terraform Cut Power by 30% vs Kubernetes
- **Pillar:** Technology
- **Score:** 7/10
- **Angle:** Michael migrated from Kubernetes + ArgoCD + Cloudflare to Docker + Terraform + Traefik, achieving 30% power reduction. He can share the exact trade-offs and operational impacts of simplifying infrastructure.
- **Trending Signal:**
  - Buildwhat.org's "Cost-Optimized Local LLM Inference Appliance" (Mar 2026) - High-memory consumer hardware undercutting cloud GPU rental costs
  - Starryhope.com's guide on Strix Halo for local LLM inference (Apr 2026) - Memory bandwidth advantages over discrete GPUs
  - Hardware-corner.net: "AMD Ryzen AI Max+ (Strix Halo) Gets Two New SKUs for Local LLM Systems" (Mar 2026)
  - Multiple developer discussions on Terraform + Docker simplification vs Kubernetes complexity in homelab contexts
- **Related Expertise:** Homelab migration (Kubernetes to Docker, 30% power reduction), Terraform IaC for homelab

---

**File Location:** `/a0/usr/workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_2026-04-09.md`

All topics avoid overlap with published content and connect directly to Michael's hands-on experience. Scores reflect search demand, uniqueness of angle, and freshness of trending signals.
